title: Decanter Centrifuge Rebuild: From Worn to Rebuilt
problem: The decanter arrived worn and out of service, showing the surface erosion, worn wear surfaces, and degraded rotating components typical of a machine due for a full rebuild. The before image documents its condition on arrival; the after image documents the same machine following a complete shop rebuild.
scopeOfWork: Non-destructive teardown and inspection (NDTI) with a documented condition report
Conveyor scroll refurbishment and hardfacing of wear surfaces
Bearing replacement on the rotating assembly
Weld repair of areas damaged by mechanical, chemical, or erosion attack
Refitting and replacement of wear parts, o-rings, and gaskets
Static and dynamic balancing of the rotating assembly
Full test run prior to return to service

after.alt: The same decanter centrifuge after a complete rebuild and reconditioning at Centrifuge World

title: Bird HB-2500 Decanter Centrifuge Rebuild
problem: A Bird HB-2500 decanter centrifuge was removed from service for a complete rebuild. The unit shows the wear pattern typical of a solids-handling decanter that has reached the end of a service interval and requires reconditioning of its rotating assembly.
scopeOfWork: Teardown, inspection, and documented condition report
Reconditioning of the bowl and conveyor assembly
Weld repair of eroded and worn areas
Wear-part replacement and refitting
Balancing and test run before return to service

title: Alfa Laval Lynx 40 Centrifuge Rebuild
problem: An Alfa Laval Lynx 40 decanter centrifuge came into the shop for repair and reconditioning. The machine required inspection and restoration of worn components to return it to reliable separation performance.
scopeOfWork: Inspection and documented condition report
Reconditioning and repair of worn components
Wear-part replacement and refitting
Balancing and testing prior to return to service

Wear-part replacement and refitting

title: Sharples Centrifuge Repair and Rebuild
problem: A Sharples centrifuge was received for repair and rebuild after losing separation performance. As with most Sharples units returning for service, worn bearings, eroded surfaces, and an out-of-balance rotating assembly called for a documented inspection followed by a full shop rebuild.
scopeOfWork: Non-destructive teardown and inspection (NDTI) with a documented condition report
Bowl and conveyor reconditioning
Weld repair of mechanical, chemical, and erosion damage
Wear-part replacement (o-rings, gaskets, bearings)
In-house fabrication of hard-to-source parts as needed
Static and dynamic balancing plus a full test run
